hero.headline: MUJ Online MBA in BFSI: Banking, Financial Services and Insurance Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved Super/Dual specialization. 12 spec subjects in Sem 3-4. NAAC A+, NIRF #58.

sections.tldr: MUJ Online MBA in BFSI covers commercial banking, insurance and risk management, investment banking, wealth management, financial services and markets, international financial management, treasury management, and FinTech in BFSI for professionals targeting branch head, relationship manager, or financial services director roles. NAAC A+, NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000 upfront (15% off) or Rs 1,80,000. Super/Dual spec — pair with Finance or IT and FinTech. Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 (70% off).

about.body:
BFSI (Banking, Financial Services and Insurance) is the MBA specialization for professionals in India's financial services sector: bank branch managers, insurance relationship managers, wealth advisors, investment banking analysts, and treasury executives who want to move into Branch Head, VP Wealth Management, or Financial Services Director roles. The MUJ Online MBA in BFSI covers the full financial services landscape: commercial banking operations, insurance risk and products, investment banking processes, wealth management, financial markets, international financial management, treasury operations, and FinTech disruption in BFSI.

MUJ's Super/Dual specialization creates differentiated BFSI profiles. Pairing BFSI with Finance creates a comprehensive corporate and institutional finance credential for roles at investment banks, mutual funds, and NBFCs. Pairing with IT and FinTech creates a digital banking and financial technology profile for BFSI digital transformation roles. Both combinations align with the broad hiring profiles of large BFSI conglomerates recruiting general management talent.

The Coursera Plus bundle at Rs 3,999 gives BFSI students access to financial markets, investment management, and FinTech courses from Yale University, University of Michigan, and Johns Hopkins — internationally recognised learning that complements the MUJ BFSI curriculum.

Manipal University Jaipur Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #58 University ranking for 2025. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from MUJ. Full programme overview at /universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Strategic planning in financial services, competitive dynamics in BFSI, and business model innovation in banking and insurance.

sem3Subjects.name: Term Paper
sem3Subjects.description: Independent research paper applying BFSI theory to a real-world banking, insurance, or financial services challenge.

sem3Subjects.name: Commercial Banking
sem3Subjects.description: Credit appraisal, working capital finance, retail and SME lending, NPA management, and RBI banking regulations.

sem3Subjects.name: Insurance and Risk Management
sem3Subjects.description: Life and non-life insurance products, actuarial principles, IRDAI regulations, risk assessment, and claims management.

sem3Subjects.name: Investment Banking
sem3Subjects.description: Equity capital markets, debt capital markets, M&A advisory, IPO process, and valuation techniques used in investment banking.

sem3Subjects.name: Wealth Management
sem3Subjects.description: Investment advisory process, asset allocation, mutual fund analysis, portfolio construction, and HNI client relationship management.

sem4Subjects.name: International Business Management
sem4Subjects.description: Cross-border banking operations, foreign currency management, and financial services strategy in international markets.

sem4Subjects.name: Project
sem4Subjects.description: Major dissertation applying BFSI specialization to an original applied banking, insurance, or financial services study.

sem4Subjects.name: Financial Services and Markets
sem4Subjects.description: Capital markets structure, SEBI regulations, derivatives markets, bond markets, and financial intermediation in India.

sem4Subjects.name: International Financial Management
sem4Subjects.description: FX risk management, cross-border capital structure, trade finance instruments, and repatriation of funds management.

sem4Subjects.name: Treasury Management
sem4Subjects.description: Corporate treasury operations, ALM in banks, liquidity management, forex dealing room operations, and CRR and SLR management.

sem4Subjects.name: Fintech in BFSI
sem4Subjects.description: Digital lending platforms, payments disruption, insurtech, wealthtech, and RBI regulatory sandbox for FinTech innovation.

syllabus.note: Semester 1 and 2 subjects are shared across all MUJ Online MBA specializations. Full programme structure at /universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba.

whoHires.body:
BFSI MBA graduates from Manipal University Jaipur Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Commercial banking, Private and wealth banking, Insurance companies, NBFCs, FinTech and digital lending

**Entry-level roles:** Relationship Manager (Banking), Insurance Advisor, Investment Analyst, Treasury Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Branch Manager, Regional Relationship Manager, Wealth Manager, Credit Manager, Insurance Product Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Zonal Head (Banking), VP Wealth Management, Head of Treasury, CFO (NBFC), Director Financial Services

**Top hiring organisations:** HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Axis Bank, SBI, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Bajaj Finserv, LIC, HDFC Life, ICICI Prudential, Aditya Birla Capital

skills.body:
The BFSI specialization builds skills that banking, insurance, and financial services employers test at branch manager and financial services director levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Credit Analysis: financial statement analysis for lending, working capital assessment, loan structuring, and NPA recognition frameworks
- Wealth Management: portfolio construction using MPT, mutual fund due diligence, risk profiling for HNI clients, and SEBI advisory regulations
- Treasury Operations: forex spot and forward dealing, ALM gap analysis, interbank market operations, and regulatory reserve management
- Insurance: product pricing fundamentals, loss ratio analysis, claims reserve estimation, and IRDAI compliance for insurance distribution

**Applied skills:**
- Investment Banking: comparable company analysis, DCF valuation, debt structuring for M&A, and pitch book preparation
- FinTech in BFSI: digital lending underwriting, open banking API strategy, and BFSI product innovation using fintech partnerships

**Soft skills:** Presenting credit proposals to credit committees, managing HNI client portfolios under regulatory frameworks, negotiating insurance program structures, and communicating treasury risk positions to CFOs and boards.

comparisons.body:
Comparing MUJ Online MBA BFSI against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Banking and Finance:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity covers banking and finance management in depth. MUJ BFSI covers the full BFSI value chain including insurance, wealth management, and FinTech disruption — a broader industry credential. For professionals moving across BFSI segments (banking to wealth to insurance), MUJ's breadth is an advantage. Amity NIRF #22 is stronger for corporate finance and investment banking careers.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Finance:** NMIMS is NAAC A++ and NIRF Management #24 at Rs 1,96,000-2,20,000. NMIMS Finance covers corporate finance, risk, and financial markets. MUJ BFSI is specifically built for financial services sector professionals — commercial banking operations, IRDAI insurance compliance, and FinTech in BFSI are not in NMIMS Finance. For BFSI sector professionals, MUJ's spec is more directly applied. NMIMS brand carries more weight at Tier 1 investment banks.

**vs LPU Online MBA Finance:** LPU is NAAC A++ with finance-oriented MBA spec. MUJ's dedicated BFSI specialization covering insurance, wealth management, treasury, and FinTech in a single spec is a differentiated credential for banking sector professionals that LPU's general finance spec does not replicate.

fees.body:
The total program fee for the Manipal University Jaipur Online MBA in BFSI can be paid through 2 options:

**Option 1 — Semester-wise payment**: Rs 45,000 per semester, Rs 1,80,000 total across 4 semesters
**Option 2 — Full upfront payment (discounted)**: Rs 1,53,000 one-time (Rs 1,80,000 standard less 15 percent fee concession)

A one-time registration fee of Rs 500 applies at enrolment. MUJ does not charge separate exam fees per attempt, project evaluation fees, or admission processing fees beyond this.

For working professionals, MUJ offers a 24-month zero-cost EMI plan at approximately Rs 7,500 per month on the standard fee (Rs 6,375 per month on the discounted fee) through its NBFC lending partners.

**Scholarships available** (non-stackable — pick one based on eligibility):
- **Divyaang** (persons with disabilities): 20% discount with Divyaang certificate
- **Defence personnel**: 20% discount with Service/IC number
- **Manipal University Jaipur alumni** (UG or PG): 20% discount
- **Government employees**: 10% discount with Govt ID
- **Merit scholarship**: 10% discount for 80%+ marks in 10+2 or bachelor's

**Coursera Plus bundled access** (MUJ differentiator): Enrolled MUJ students get Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 (standard Rs 13,999 — a 70 percent discount). This includes 10,000+ courses from University of Virginia, University of Michigan, Johns Hopkins, University of California Irvine, and other top global universities.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees and scholarships with Manipal University Jaipur each quarter. Please reconfirm current offers and eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Manipal University Jaipur degree certificate carries the Manipal University Jaipur name, the MBA designation, and identifies the online learning mode per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The BFSI specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode identification on the certificate is standard for UGC-DEB approved programmes and equivalent in recognition to regular MBA degrees for employment, promotions, and further studies.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from MUJ Online MBA BFSI alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Ravi Agarwal
items.liked: Treasury management and investment banking subjects gave me frameworks I had been missing in 7 years of banking operations — directly applicable to my corporate banking role
items.disliked: Derivatives and structured products content was introductory — not sufficient for fixed income or structured credit roles at institutional banks
items.body: I work in corporate banking at a private sector bank. The treasury, international financial management, and investment banking subjects were the most applicable content I have encountered outside formal banking training. Paired BFSI with Finance for a comprehensive banking and corporate finance credential. The Manipal brand was recognised at HDFC Bank and Kotak for my senior relationship manager application. The Coursera Plus Yale financial markets course at Rs 3,999 was excellent for building capital markets context.

items.name: Sunita Reddy
items.liked: Insurance and risk management subject covered IRDAI product regulations in enough depth to be directly useful in my insurance company role
items.disliked: Exam scheduling was problematic — proctoring technical issues during my treasury management paper required a re-attempt that delayed my semester result
items.body: I manage insurance product distribution at a life insurance company. The insurance, wealth management, and FinTech in BFSI subjects were directly applicable. Paired BFSI with IT and FinTech to build a digital insurance distribution credential for my company's digital initiatives. The Manipal NAAC A+ was verified without issues by our parent group HR. Career support was not relevant since I joined for the credential, not placement.

items.name: Mahesh Kumar
items.liked: Wealth management subject gave good portfolio construction and client profiling frameworks for my mutual fund advisory practice
items.disliked: Placement support was not useful for BFSI sector roles. Career services had no connections with private banks, NBFCs, or insurance companies
items.body: The content itself was good across the BFSI subjects. Commercial banking, wealth management, and financial markets were thorough and practically framed. My frustration was career services — the online programme placement team had no connections with banking or insurance hiring managers. My two job changes since completing the MBA were entirely through LinkedIn and banking industry events. Join for the credential and BFSI-specific knowledge. Do not join expecting sector-specific placement support.

faqs.question: Can I choose dual specialization with the MUJ Online MBA in BFSI?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Super/Dual model lets you pair BFSI with a second specialization at no extra cost. Strong combinations: BFSI plus Finance (for corporate finance and investment banking credential), BFSI plus IT and FinTech (for digital banking and payments technology roles), or BFSI plus Analytics and Data Science (for risk analytics and credit scoring roles at banks and NBFCs). Both specializations appear on your marksheet.

faqs.question: Is Coursera Plus access included or charged separately?
faqs.answer: Coursera Plus is available to enrolled MUJ students at Rs 3,999 per year (standard Rs 13,999 — 70% off). For BFSI students, valuable Coursera offerings include Financial Markets by Yale University, Investment Management from Geneva University, and FinTech courses from University of Michigan. These complement the MUJ BFSI curriculum and are globally recognised on professional profiles.

faqs.question: What scholarships are available for the MUJ Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Five scholarship categories: 20% for Divyaang individuals, 20% for Defence personnel, 20% for MUJ alumni, 10% for Government employees, and 10% merit scholarship for 80%+ marks in 10+2 or bachelor's. Non-stackable. Plus: upfront payment of the full fee gets a 15% fee concession — Rs 1,80,000 becomes Rs 1,53,000.

faqs.question: What salary can BFSI MBA graduates from MUJ expect?
faqs.answer: Branch managers, wealth managers, and relationship managers with 4-7 years of experience and this MBA earn Rs 12-24 lakh at private banks and NBFCs. Zonal heads and VP Wealth Management roles pay Rs 20-40 lakh. CFO and director roles at mid-size NBFCs and insurance companies pay Rs 30-60 lakh. Outcomes depend strongly on the specific BFSI segment (retail banking vs investment banking vs insurance), company brand, and location.

faqs.question: Is this MBA relevant for bank employees seeking promotion to managerial grades?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of the primary use cases for the BFSI specialization. Bank employees in clerical and officer grades use this degree to qualify for management promotion in private sector banks that require a formal postgraduate credential. The commercial banking, treasury, and credit management content is directly applicable to banking promotions. Public sector banks (PSBs) have their own internal promotion processes, but the MBA credential is increasingly considered for internal management grade promotions.

chooseIf: You work in banking, insurance, wealth management, or treasury and want a NAAC A+ MBA with dedicated BFSI coverage including commercial banking, insurance, investment banking, and FinTech under Rs 1.8 lakh.
You want to pair BFSI with Finance or IT and FinTech for a corporate finance or digital banking credential using MUJ's Super/Dual spec.
You value Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 for Yale Financial Markets, investment management, or FinTech courses that complement the BFSI curriculum.
consider: You are targeting investment banking or capital markets roles at Tier 1 firms — NMIMS Finance or CFA is more relevant for Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, and Kotak IB hiring at the analyst and associate levels.
You need strong actuarial or insurance-specific credentials — IRDA-licensed certifications or ACII are required for senior insurance product and underwriting roles beyond what an MBA provides.
You need active banking sector placement support — MUJ online career services do not maintain connections with private bank HR or NBFC hiring teams.
